planner: Claude Code (self-planned; no separate ChatGPT planner session was active — the master prompt explicitly authorizes updating ai-workflow files per repo convention when this happens)
objective: Bring every IGNYT screen into one coherent premium design system matching the supplied light-theme reference (cool blue-gray bg, white cards, blue primary accent) with an adapted dark theme, plus the explicit functional restructuring requirements in REQUEST.md, without losing any existing feature or data.
current_git_status_at_plan_time: Clean tracked tree; untracked: AGENTS.md, ai-workflow/, exercise-image-generator/, ignyt-image-generator-input.zip, ignyt-references.zip.

superseded_task.note: Approved (PLAN.json implementation_authorized:true) but not yet implemented (only ai-workflow scaffolding existed). Its own PLAN.json content is preserved in git history (this commit's parent) and can be restored/resumed after this task.

reference_image_findings.global: Light bg ~near-white cool blue-gray, white elevated cards (radius ~16-20px), thin light border, subtle shadow, bright blue (~#2F6FED-class) primary accent for active nav/buttons/rings, dark navy/near-black text, gray secondary text, small blue accent line + uppercase 'TRAIN WITH INTENT' + bold 'IGNYT' wordmark header, circular theme-toggle button top-right.
reference_image_findings.bottom_nav: Home / Workout / Nutrition / Progress / AI Coach, white bg, active = filled blue icon + blue label, inactive = muted gray.
reference_image_findings.home: Greeting + name + streak (existing dynamic logic, reuse verbatim) with athlete image top-right (already implemented in a prior session). 'TODAY'S PROGRESS' section: ring showing '33% GOAL' with subtitle 'Week 1 of 8 · BASE' + two stat rows: Calories (bolt icon) and Steps (chart icon) -- see resolved_conflicts.home_progress_metrics below for why Protein was NOT carried over from this screenshot.
reference_image_findings.workout: Full-width blue 'Start Empty Workout' button, then 'Recent Sessions' + 'Show All (N)' link, session cards with muscle-diagram avatar, title, exercise count/duration, muscle chips, date, PR badge, delete icon. NOTE: this screenshot's own subtext ('Looking for your routines? They've moved to the Plan tab') describes CURRENT app copy being redesigned away, not a target -- REQUEST.md Section K/Y explicitly require routines to live ON the Workout page, which is the opposite. Written requirement wins (see resolved_conflicts.routines_location). Screenshot also shows 5 recent sessions; written requirement explicitly caps this at exactly 2 (Section X) -- written requirement wins on the count, screenshot wins on the per-card visual design.
reference_image_findings.progress: 'Progress' title + subtitle 'Your training, body and performance insights'. 'THIS WEEK' card (Workouts, Training Time, Weekly Volume, Current Streak rows). 'EXPLORE' section: Personal Records / Achievements / Workout Analytics / Exercise Progress row cards (icon badge, title, subtitle, chevron).
reference_image_findings.workout_analytics_detail: Range chips (7D/4W/12W/6M/1Y), 'Training Volume' + %-change badge, blue line chart with light fill, 4-stat grid (Workouts/Avg Duration/Calories Burned/PRs) each with a %-change badge.
reference_image_findings.nutrition_overview: EATEN vs BURNED (EST.) cards, Calories Remaining row, big 'DEFICIT CREATED' card, Macronutrients Today (Protein/Carbs/Fat/Fibre bars), Water bar + quick-add pill buttons.
reference_image_findings.nutrition_meals: Overview/Meals/Insights segmented tabs; 3 macro rings; Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner/Snacks rows with icon badges and + Add Meal.
reference_image_findings.start_workout: Contextual header, 'Start Workout' title, selected routine card (muscle diagram, chest/shoulders/triceps, 5 exercises · ~60 min, Change Workout), prominent Start button, numbered exercise rows with icon + sets/reps + drag handle.
reference_image_findings.ai_coach_empty: Rounded blue icon badge with '•••', 'AI Coach' title, honest 'AI coaching is not configured...' copy (already matches the app's existing honest empty-state philosophy), 'Explore your progress' secondary button.
reference_image_findings.ai_coach_chat: Robot avatar, greeting bubble, 4 suggested-prompt rows with icon + chevron, bottom input bar with send button -- only relevant if/when a real backend exists; otherwise the existing empty state already satisfies this page's intent.

resolved_conflicts.screenshot_shows: Ring (33% GOAL) + Calories + Protein + Steps (3 stat rows including a diet-intake metric).
resolved_conflicts.written_requirement_says: Section J: 'Keep ONLY: Steps. Total Calories Burned. Remove from this specific Home progress card: Protein... Do not calculate misleading combined progress percentages from unrelated metrics.'
resolved_conflicts.resolution: Written functional requirement wins (AJ priority order scopes the screenshot's priority to *visual* decisions; metric inclusion is a functional/data decision). Ring reverts to the single genuine 'planPct' (weekly plan completion %) it used before an earlier session's pass blended it with diet metrics -- this also happens to match the screenshot's own ring subtitle 'Week 1 of 8 · BASE', which only makes sense for plan-completion, not a diet/activity blend. Stat rows become exactly two: Steps (existing Health Connect read) and Total Calories Burned (reuse existing todayBurned(), which already correctly sources HC active calories + maintenance estimate -- same function Nutrition's 'BURNED (EST.)' card already uses). Calories-eaten and Protein rows removed from this card entirely (not fabricated as 0, genuinely removed).

resolved_conflicts.screenshot_shows: Copy reading 'Looking for your routines? They've moved to the Plan tab.'
resolved_conflicts.written_requirement_says: Section K: 'Move workout routines to the Workout page.' Section Y: 'Workout routines must be primarily accessible from the Workout page. Do not keep routines primarily hidden inside Plan.'
resolved_conflicts.resolution: Written requirement wins -- the screenshot is documenting the CURRENT (pre-redesign) copy that Section K/Y explicitly instruct reversing. Plan tab keeps whatever distinct purpose it serves (HYROX schedule structure) per 'Preserve existing Plan functionality that serves a distinct purpose'; routine start/create/edit/delete moves onto the main Workout page.

phases.name: Design foundation: light theme (blue primary) + dark theme adaptation
phases.scope: Extend www/css/tokens.css: promote a blue primary accent for interactive elements matching the reference (new --color-interactive / reuse --color-accent-blue as the primary interactive color), redefine [data-theme="light"] to the reference's cool blue-gray bg / white cards / blue accent, keep dark theme conceptually aligned (same tokens, dark values) without breaking the current dark aesthetic users already have. CSS-only, additive, no HTML/JS changes.

phases.name: Home: Today's Progress simplification
phases.scope: www/js/pages/home.js: ring reverts to planPct (with documented rationale in code comment), stat rows reduce to Steps + Total Calories Burned (reuse todayBurned()), remove Calories-eaten/Protein from this card. www/app.js: pass burned (todayBurned()) into the Home ctx.

phases.name: Remove Body Fat % from Log Weight UI
phases.scope: www/app.js renderBodyTab: remove the 'Body Fat (%)' input from the Log Entry form grid and the corresponding read/write in the log-body click handler. Historical bodyfat data, the standalone Body Fat calculator, and the waist-based Navy-method estimate fallback are all left completely intact. No standalone 'Log Weight' destination exists separately from the body/Profile tab today, so that part of Section N is already satisfied by the existing architecture.

phases.name: Workout restructuring (routines on Workout page, 2-session cap + Show All, rest timer default 0)
phases.scope_actually_done: Moved the 'My Routines' section (list/create/edit/delete/start, including renderRoutineBuilder and its exercise-picker routing check) from renderPlanTab into www/js/pages/workout.js's renderWorkoutList, in the order Section K specifies: Start Empty Workout -> My Routines -> Recent Sessions. Plan tab keeps HYROX Training Schedule + Race Simulation (its distinct purpose) plus a pointer note to Workout. Session list default cap changed from 5 to 2 (state.workoutLog.slice(0,2)); Show All still renders the complete stored history, nothing deleted from storage. state.settings.defaultRest changed from 90 to 0 -- verified safe with zero migration needed: Object.assign({defaultRest:0,...}, LS.get('hx_settings',{})) means any existing installation already has defaultRest explicitly persisted in hx_settings (persist() writes settings every render), so the code-level default change only affects brand-new installs with no hx_settings yet; already-created exercises/routines keep whatever restDuration value was baked into them at creation time, untouched.
phases.not_done: Start Workout screen's own visual redesign (numbered exercise rows with drag handle, muscle-diagram header per the reference) was not attempted -- the underlying screen (renderPlanTab's old flow doesn't apply; the equivalent is the active-session header in renderWorkoutTab, part of the still-un-extracted ~1,600-line active-session renderer) needs its own dedicated pass.
phases.browser_verified: Full routine lifecycle tested end-to-end in a real browser session on a fresh install: create routine -> open exercise picker from Workout tab -> pick exercise -> return to builder -> save -> routine appears in My Routines on Workout tab (not Plan) -> Start Workout -> session starts with the new exercise showing 'Rest Timer: OFF' (confirms defaultRest:0 took effect) -> localStorage hx_settings.defaultRest confirmed 0. Plan tab confirmed to no longer show routines. Zero console errors, zero horizontal overflow throughout.

phases.name: Profile consolidation: Personal Records + Achievements entry points, body-progress photo storage
phases.body_progress_photos: COMPLETED. New window.IgnytBodyPhotosDB module (www/js/body-photos-db.js, IndexedDB, database 'ignyt-body-photos') with addPhoto/getAllMeta/getBlob/deletePhoto -- every method resolves/rejects a Promise, never throws synchronously, so a missing/broken IndexedDB degrades to an empty photo list rather than crashing. state.bodyPhotos holds metadata only (no blobs) as an in-memory cache, populated once asynchronously right after the initial boot render (first paint never waits on it) and refreshed after every add/delete. Actual image blobs are fetched lazily per-thumbnail via a module-level object-URL cache (photoThumbUrl()) so accounts with many photos never hold every blob in memory simultaneously. UI in Profile: category chips (Front/Side/Back/Other), optional note, a native file input styled as the existing .btn-accent (accept='image/*', so mobile browsers/WebView offer camera or gallery), a 3-column thumbnail grid (newest first), and a lightbox for viewing/deleting a single photo. Edge cases handled: cancelled file selection (input.files empty -> silent no-op), non-image files (MIME-type check -> toast), IndexedDB unavailable (module methods reject -> toast, feature just shows empty). Deliberately used IndexedDB over @capacitor/filesystem (see prior note -- avoids the Kotlin 1.9.24 conflict entirely, zero native/Gradle changes).
phases.bug_found_and_fixed_during_verification: The photo lightbox originally reused the app's shared .dialog-backdrop/.dialog-box/.dialog-actions classes for its own Close/Delete buttons. Browser testing caught a real conflict: deleting a photo from inside the lightbox opens the app's actual confirm dialog (which uses the exact same classes) ON TOP of the still-open lightbox -- both were landing at the same default z-index (210/211 from the CSS class), so which one visually won was arbitrary/collided, and a naive class-based button query (as used in this session's first test attempt) matched the wrong element. Fixed by keeping the shared classes for backdrop/box visual styling (dimming, shape) but explicitly setting the lightbox's z-index to 190/191 (below the confirm dialog's 210/211) and replacing the button row's shared .dialog-actions class with unstyled inline layout, so there's no more ambiguity in either stacking order or handler targeting. Re-verified with the confirm dialog's real data-dialog-action="confirm" selector: delete now genuinely works end-to-end.
phases.browser_verified: Full lifecycle tested with a real synthetic image blob (not a placeholder path): add via simulated file input -> saved to IndexedDB -> grid re-renders with a lazy-loaded real blob: URL thumbnail -> open lightbox -> delete -> confirm dialog appears correctly on top and is clickable -> confirm -> photo removed from IndexedDB and the grid reverts to its honest empty state. Zero console errors, zero horizontal overflow at 320px throughout.

phases.name: Progress: Habit Tracker (new feature) + remove redundant Nutrition Progress content
phases.habit_tracker: New feature, self-contained: state.habits (hx_habits) + state.habitCompletions (hx_habit_completions), two new persisted keys, no migration needed (brand new feature, no prior data). Added 'habits' as a new PROGRESS_VIEWS entry (reuses the exact same Explore-grid/detail-view navigation pattern as PRs/Achievements/Analytics -- no new UI paradigm). Full CRUD: add (inline name input), rename (click name -> inline edit -> Save), delete (existing confirmDialog() pattern, same as workout-session delete), toggle today's completion (reuses the existing .set-check component). Per-habit stats: current streak, best streak (longest historical run), this-week completion (0-7), this-month completion count. Deliberately does NOT reuse the app's existing todayStr()/computeStreak() date pattern (new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10) on a local-midnight Date, which is UTC-shifted and off-by-one for any non-zero-offset timezone at local midnight -- confirmed present in computeStreak() too) -- wrote a new habitDateStr() that correctly adjusts for local timezone offset before formatting, per the explicit 'avoid streak-calculation bugs caused by local timezone versus UTC' requirement. The pre-existing pattern elsewhere in the app was left untouched (out of scope -- it touches historical data keys across food/body/workout logging).
phases.nutrition_progress_redundancy: Investigated renderProgressNutrition() (the Progress page's existing 'Nutrition Progress' category): it shows 30/60/90-day average-calories/protein-per-logged-day and sparkline trends -- historical/trend analytics, genuinely distinct from the real-time Nutrition tab's today's-macros/meals/water view. No actual duplication was found. Per 'do not remove functionality just because it is not shown in the reference image' and 'Preserve every existing IGNYT feature', nothing was removed here -- this is a documented interpretation per the ambiguity-resolution instruction, not a skipped requirement.
phases.browser_verified: Full lifecycle tested in a real browser session: add habit -> toggle complete (streak 0->1, week 0/7->1/7, month 0->1, persisted hx_habit_completions with a correct local-date key and genuine timestamp) -> toggle off (streak reverts to 0) -> rename (inline edit -> Save, name updates) -> delete (confirmDialog shows correct message, confirms, habit + its history removed, hx_habits back to []). Zero console errors, zero horizontal overflow throughout.

phases.name: Calculator consolidation review + remaining page visual consistency
phases.calculator_consolidation: Verified already satisfied by the existing architecture -- renderCalculators() is defined once (app.js ~4366) and called exactly once (inside renderBodyTab / the Profile tab, ~5354). No duplicated or scattered calculator content exists; nothing to consolidate. 'Lock section' ambiguity resolved: no locked calculator section exists anywhere in the current codebase, so there was nothing to preserve-in-place; documented here per the ambiguity-resolution instruction, no fabricated feature added.
phases.visual_consistency: The core design system was already centralized and shared across every screen (www/css/tokens.css + base/layout/components/responsive + per-page css) before this task. This phase extended the reference's bright-blue interactive accent to the remaining legacy selection components that still read the old orange/steel palette, via the components.css override layer (no inline-stylesheet or markup changes): .cat-chip.active, .week-chip.active (+ its inner bar), and .day-tab.active now use --color-interactive with white text, matching the reference's selected-chip look. Deliberately NOT repainted (documented, not skipped): --mint completion checks (.set-check.done/.ex-check.done) stay green (success semantics, not selection), and the orange --color-primary brand highlights (streak flame, PR/achievement accents) stay orange -- the earlier standing instruction to 'preserve the IGNYT orange identity' plus the master prompt's priority #3 ('existing IGNYT branding') make a full orange->blue repaint wrong; blue is applied to interactive/selected states only, which is exactly what the reference uses it for. Also verified the legacy --steel token is already a blue (#4FA8D8), so components already using it (search bars, some labels) were already consistent with the blue direction.
phases.browser_verified: week-chip.active, day-tab.active, cat-chip.active all confirmed rgb(62,130,247) with white text via getComputedStyle in dark theme; cat-chip.active confirmed still blue in light theme; streak confirmed still orange rgb(255,90,31) (brand preserved). Full Progress detail-view sweep (all 9 views incl. new Habit Tracker) rendered without error with working back-nav; Library/Settings/Health Connect/Nutrition/AI Coach all render without error or overflow; persistence-after-reload confirmed (habit + defaultRest:0 + onboarding survive a reload). Light theme confirmed applying the reference's cool blue-gray bg (#f4f6fa). Zero console errors across the entire sweep, zero horizontal overflow at 320px.

phases.name: Regression validation, Android build, final report
phases.scope: Full cross-screen regression sweep performed in-browser this session (Home, Workout incl. routines/active session, Nutrition, Progress + all 9 detail views, Profile incl. body photos + PR/Achievement entries + Habit Tracker, Plan/HYROX schedule, Library, Settings, Health Connect, AI Coach) -- all render without error or overflow, zero console errors, persistence survives reload. Final npx cap sync android + gradlew clean assembleDebug run for a from-scratch build verification. Structured final report delivered to the user.

git_requirements: Commit locally after each safely-verified phase (git safety baseline already established across this branch's history).

Do NOT push without explicit user authorization -- AGENTS.md Section 9 ('Always: Do not commit or push unless explicitly authorized by the workflow') is stricter than WORKFLOW.md's blanket allowance and governs.
Never stage node_modules/, android build outputs, APKs, or secrets.
note_on_pacing: This is an exceptionally large multi-phase request (7 phases, several genuinely new features). Consistent with this branch's established history (one safely-verified increment per session) and with the master prompt's own 'prefer incremental, testable implementation' instruction, phases are being implemented and verified in order across multiple work sessions rather than as one unverified batch. Phase status above reflects true current progress, updated honestly as work proceeds.
